page: Google Cloud Platform c3-highcpu-192-metal is a compute-optimized bare-metal server powered by an Intel Xeon 8481C processor. This x86_64 architecture system features 96 physical cores and 192 vCPUs with hyper-threading enabled, running at a clock speed of 4.0 GHz. The processor includes a 105 MB L3 cache and supports advanced instruction sets such as AVX-512 and Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions for tile and int8 operations. The server is equipped with 512 GB of DDR5 memory operating at 5 GHz, providing 2.67 GB of RAM per core. No local storage or GPUs are included in this configuration.

Benchmark results place this server in the top tier for multi-core processing and memory bandwidth. The stress-ng multi-core CPU test and PassMark CPU score both rank in the top 10 percent, reflecting the capabilities of the 192 dedicated vCPUs. Memory performance is also highly rated, with read-only small block cached bandwidth reaching 13145.74 GB/sec and large block uncached write bandwidth at 190.86 GB/sec. In database and web serving tests, the server achieved 184.72 million operations per second in Redis SET benchmarks and 126.66 GB/sec in static web serving throughput. However, single-core performance is more moderate, with stress-ng single-core and Geekbench text processing scoring in the average and weak tiers, respectively.

Given its bare-metal architecture and high compute density, this server offers qualitative cost efficiency for workloads that require direct hardware access and massive parallel processing without hypervisor overhead. The high core count and advanced vector and matrix extensions make it suitable for compute-heavy tasks, software compilation, database operations, and high-throughput web serving. The lack of local storage means external storage solutions must be utilized, which is a key architectural tradeoff to consider.
description: Google Cloud Platform c3-highcpu-192-metal is a compute-optimized bare-metal server designed for high-throughput parallel workloads. It features an Intel Xeon 8481C x86_64 processor with 96 physical cores, 192 dedicated vCPUs running at 4.0 GHz, and 512 GB of DDR5 RAM. The hardware supports advanced instruction sets like AVX-512 and Intel AMX. In benchmark testing, the server demonstrates top-tier multi-core CPU performance, high memory bandwidth, and exceptional database and web serving capabilities, including 184.72 million Redis SET operations per second. However, single-core performance and text processing benchmarks are relatively moderate. Lacking local storage and GPUs, this bare-metal instance is best suited for compute-intensive applications, large-scale software compilation, and high-volume database operations that benefit from direct hardware access.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform c3-highcpu-192-metal is a bare-metal server featuring 192 vCPUs, an Intel Xeon 4.0 GHz processor, and 512 GB of DDR5 RAM, optimized for high-performance parallel computing.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform c3-highcpu-192-metal is a compute-optimized bare-metal server with 192 vCPUs, 512 GB DDR5 RAM, and Intel Xeon processors.
tagline: A high-density, bare-metal compute platform featuring 192 dedicated vCPUs and DDR5 memory for intensive parallel workloads.
bullet_points: Intel Xeon 8481C processor with 192 vCPUs and 4.0 GHz clock speed
512 GB of high-speed 5 GHz DDR5 memory
Top-tier multi-core CPU and memory bandwidth benchmark performance
Supports Intel AVX-512 and Advanced Matrix Extensions
Bare-metal architecture with no hypervisor overhead
Best suited for parallel compute, software compilation, and database workloads
